title: Private key PEM strings not zeroized on drop
description:
Throughout the codebase, RSA and ECDSA private key PEM strings are held in plain String values that are never scrubbed from memory on drop:

- delivery.rs line 21: DeliveryRow.private_key_pem (String)
- federation.rs line 470: private_key_pem parameter (held as &str, but the underlying String in callers)
- cli.rs line 481: private_key_pem (Zeroizing<String> from rsa crate, BUT bound into sqlx as .as_str() — the PEM is also copied into the DB query buffer unzeroed)
- push.rs line 65: VAPID pem fetched as String from DB
- inbox.rs line 351: private_key_pem fetched as String from DB

The rsa crate's RsaPrivateKey does implement Zeroize, so the parsed key struct is scrubbed. But the PEM-encoded String copies persist in memory until the allocator reuses the page.

Impact: in a memory-dump scenario (core dump, /proc/pid/mem, cold-boot attack), an attacker can recover key material from heap.

Fix: use secrecy::SecretString or zeroize::Zeroizing<String> for all variables holding PEM content. Ensure the DB driver does not leave unzeroed copies (may require a wrapper).
